frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Sach bolun, maa ke haath ke simple dal-chawal mein bhi jo sukoon hota hai na, woh kisi restaurant ya ready-to-eat mein nahi milta. Puri zindagi bina complain kiye family ke liye itna kuch karti hain, tabhi ghar ghar lagta hai. Bas isi liye lagta hai ki jo ladki ghar sambhalna aur pyaar se khana banana samajhti hai, wahi family ko truly jod ke rakhti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it glorifies a mother's unpaid domestic labor as her natural expression of love and sets it up as the standard for women generally. It encodes labor erasure and the implicit expectation that a 'good' woman proves her worth through cooking and homemaking.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 26. Single. A man in Delhi. You are posting anonymously on a Facebook group called Delhi Confessions after seeing a girl in a short dress at a metro station who was getting stared at by men around her. You are writing your post about what you saw and giving your opinion on why girls who dress a certain way in public spaces are responsible for the attention they get and what they should expect.
